task_updates.executor_notes: A true manual end-to-end pass for the 12 interaction paths could not be completed from this sandboxed CLI. Evidence gathered instead: `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/registration.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/lib/render-bounds.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/lib/render-bounds.validation.test.ts src/tools/video-editor/lib/serialize.test.ts` passes all 6 files / 15 tests; prior T8 build and typecheck gates are green; code-path inspection confirms the interaction wiring exists for (1) ruler click and (2) scrub via `TimeRuler` -> `useTimelineSync`, (3)-(4) resize via `TimelineCanvas` transient overrides -> `useClipResize`, (5)-(6) internal/cross-track drag via `useClipDrag` + `useDragCoordinator` + `.cross-track-ghost`, (7)-(8) external drops and upload skeletons via `useExternalDrop`, (9) new-track drop affordance via `DropIndicator` + `.timeline-canvas-edit-area.drop-target-new-track`, (10)-(11) split/delete via `useClipEditing` and clip selection/context-menu flow, and (12) playback sync via `useTimelineSync` -> `timelineRef.setTime()`. Attempting `npm run dev -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 4173` failed with `listen EPERM`, so the remaining manual validation must be done in a local interactive browser session outside this sandbox.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: I cannot honestly mark all 12 interaction paths as passed from this environment because there is no browser automation harness and manual browser interaction is unavailable; a local Vite server startup attempt is blocked by sandbox `EPERM` on `listen`. Best-effort evidence is positive but indirect: targeted video-editor tests pass (6 files / 15 tests), the build/typecheck gates are already green, and code inspection shows the new timeline implementation is wired for smooth resize, cross-track ghost rendering, external-drop skeleton creation, new-track affordances, and playback-driven cursor sync. Final pass/fail for SC9 still requires an interactive browser session outside this sandbox.
